deployment.name: Patient Portal AI Assistant
deployment.description: HIPAA-covered patient-facing assistant inside a hospital patient portal. Answers questions about appointments, medication refills, lab results, and visit prep. Authenticated users only; routes anything clinical to a nurse triage queue. Handles PHI so PII redaction + audit logging are non-negotiable.

anchor_query.example: When is my next cardiology appointment and what should I bring?

lookup.description: Pulls a record from the EHR via MCP and summarizes

informational.description: Open-ended question; full RAG against approved patient-education corpus

triage_handoff.description: Routes to a nurse; short summary for the triage queue

refusal.description: Clinical question the AI is not permitted to answer (e.g., dosing advice)

post-launch-spike.label: Post-launch spike (more refusals as patients test the system)

segments.label: Active patients
segments.description: Authenticated portal users; bot factor 1.0 because SSO + patient login gates traffic.

federal._pii_redaction_note: Re-calibrated 2026-05-12 from 22 → 0.50 /M tokens. The prior value was ~50x industry-typical PII redaction service pricing (AWS Comprehend, Azure DLP, Google DLP all run $0.20-1.50/M characters, roughly $0.05-0.40/M tokens; $0.50/M tokens is a conservative midpoint covering HIPAA-grade inline redaction). Old value alone added ~$210K/mo of phantom federal-additive cost to this preset's headline at the 2.16M-query/mo volume — making the HIPAA chatbot appear ~6x more expensive than it actually is.
